CAD/CAM Provisional Materials", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.25, "year": 2025, "authors": ["Soliman T.A."], "venue": "PMC / Preprints.org", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Thermocycling (5000 cycles, 5-55C, 30s dwell) significantly reduced shear bond strength of CAD-Temp (83-86 wt% PMMA) from 8.84 to 6.04 MPa (diamond bur group) and from 12.86 to 8.82 MPa (sandblast group), attributed to water infiltration between polymer chains causing matrix softening.", "direct_quote": "The high polymeric content (83-86 wt% PMMA) may account for this phenomenon, as it is prone to water infiltration between the gaps of the polymer chains, resulting in their separation.", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.35, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["CAD-Temp", "PMMA", "shear bond strength", "thermocycling", "water infiltration"]}, {"statement": "PEEK (polyether ether ketone) showed no significant shear bond strength reduction after 5000 thermocycling cycles in the air particle abrasion group (14.37 to 12.92 MPa, p>0.05), attributed to its low water sorption of 6.5 ug/mm3 vs CAD-Temp at 40 ug/mm3.", "direct_quote": "PEEK demonstrates a water sorption value of (<=6.5 ug/mm3), CAD-Temp presents a value of (<=40 ug/mm3), and Everest C-Temp has a value of (9.6 ug/mm3)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.3, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["PEEK", "water sorption", "shear bond strength", "thermocycling"]}, {"statement": "Three-way ANOVA showed all three factors (material type F=711.0, surface treatment F=845.6, and thermal aging F=730.9) had statistically significant effects on shear bond strength (all p<0.001), with significant interaction effects between material and aging (F=110.8, p=0.004).", "direct_quote": "The three-way analysis of variance showed significant differences in SBS values among different types of materials (F = 813.74, p < 0.001), surface treatments (F = 602.203, p < 0.001), and the aging conditions (F = 244.167, p < 0.001)", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.25, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["ANOVA", "shear bond strength", "thermal aging", "surface treatment"]}], "evidence_summary": "Dental materials study examining thermocycling (5000 cycles, 5-55C) effects on shear bond strength of CAD/CAM provisionals (CAD-Temp/PMMA, C-Temp/fiberglass, PEEK). Demonstrates that water sorption is a key predictor of bond strength degradation under thermal aging — PMMA-based materials lose 30% SBS while low-sorption PEEK is stable. Tangentially relevant to understanding polymer adhesion under moisture/thermal cycling, but not directly about DVS-PEI encapsulants or flexible electronics.
